adds.rationale: Palantir's Q4-2024 results showed a clear commercial inflection (US commercial +54% YoY, US gov +45% YoY) and the company is the cleanest public bet on AI applied to defense/intelligence software, fitting both the AI and geopolitical waves the profile emphasizes.

news_evidence.summary: Palantir Q4-2024 earnings: US commercial revenue +54% YoY, US government revenue +45% YoY; full-year 2025 revenue guide raised to $3.74B-$3.76B implying ~31% growth.
European defence stocks surge on Trump's pause of Ukraine aid and demands for NATO 5% GDP spending; AI-defense software providers are positioned for the same tailwind.

removes.rationale: XLE is a broad energy ETF whose role in the watchlist is largely redundant with LNG, which is a more focused expression of the same geopolitical-energy thesis (Russia-Ukraine driving US LNG exports); freeing the slot lets the cap-16 watchlist add a higher-conviction name without thematic loss.

news_evidence.summary: TSMC details $100B additional Arizona investment (March 4, 2025), reinforcing that the dominant Q1-25 industrial-policy story is semis/AI infrastructure, not broad oil-and-gas; XLE's relative case has weakened versus more targeted exposures.

LNG (Cheniere) remains the cleanest single-name play on the persistent Russia-Ukraine-driven US LNG export tailwind already named in the profile, making XLE's broad-energy exposure incremental rather than core.

rationale_overall: Net watchlist remains at the cap of 16. Swap one broad commodity ETF (XLE) for a higher-conviction AI/defense software name (PLTR) whose Q4-2024 results showed both commercial acceleration and continued government strength, lining up with the profile's AI-plus-geopolitical thesis. Quantum (IONQ) and nuclear (CCJ/CEG/SMR) exposures are retained despite Q1 drawdowns because the profile explicitly names them as next waves and no thesis-breaching news has appeared.
